SERIES B
Vori Raises $22 Million in Series B

Vori, a San Francisco, CA-based AI grocery startup, has raised $22 million in Series B funding, bringing its total funding to $50 million.

by
Ben Murray
Updated May 07, 2026

Vori, a San Francisco, CA-based AI grocery startup, has raised $22 million in Series B funding, bringing its total funding to $50 million.  

Investors  

The round was led by Cherryrock Capital, with participation from Greylock Partners and The Factory.  

Cherryrock Capital is a U.S.-based venture capital firm founded in 2023. The firm focuses on Series A and B investments in software and technology startups, particularly supporting founders from historically underfunded backgrounds. Its investment strategy centers on enterprise software, AI, fintech, and healthcare technology companies.  

Vori Use of Funds  

The company will use the funding to expand its AI-powered grocery operating system and modernize outdated grocery store infrastructure, including payments, inventory, ordering, and invoice management.

Brandon Hill
3w

We just raised a $22M Series B for Vori to make every supermarket in America autonomous.

Grocery is a $1.5T domestic market — bigger than restaurants, bigger than hotels. But it's running on technology from the Reagan administration. 

This industry is massive, essential, and near and dear to my heart.

My parents met in a supermarket. My grandparents owned a grocery store. My mom Tori works at Vori.

Walmart and Amazon have invested heavily in this space, capturing 25% of U.S. grocery spending. But no one is building tech for the other 75% of the market.

Vori is the engine for grocery: an AI-native operating system that handles checkout, payments, pricing, ordering, inventory, and loyalty.

→ A system of record: tracks all activity happening in the store in one place. 
→ A system of action: agents leverage what the store "knows" to actually do the work.
→ A system of transaction: Vori processes every dollar that flows through the store, accounting for 60% of our revenue.

Exclusive: AI grocery startup Vori raises $22 million to help independent retailers compete with Walmart and Amazon
Lily Mae Lazarus

WMT

Brandon Hill’s parents met and fell in love in an independent grocery store. Decades later, the startup founder is trying to make sure stores like that don’t get swallowed up by Walmart and Amazon.

Brandon Hill
3w

We just raised a $22M Series B for Vori to make every supermarket in America autonomous.

Grocery is a $1.5T domestic market — bigger than restaurants, bigger than hotels. But it's running on technology from the Reagan administration. 

This industry is massive, essential, and near and dear to my heart.

My parents met in a supermarket. My grandparents owned a grocery store. My mom Tori works at Vori.

Walmart and Amazon have invested heavily in this space, capturing 25% of U.S. grocery spending. But no one is building tech for the other 75% of the market.

Vori is the engine for grocery: an AI-native operating system that handles checkout, payments, pricing, ordering, inventory, and loyalty.

→ A system of record: tracks all activity happening in the store in one place. 
→ A system of action: agents leverage what the store "knows" to actually do the work.
→ A system of transaction: Vori processes every dollar that flows through the store, accounting for 60% of our revenue.

The future we are building with our customers and ecosystem partners is critical to ensuring greater access to the essential goods folks need in order to live, work, learn, and play–starting with food. Vori Raises $22 Million in Series B

Vori, a San Francisco, CA-based AI grocery startup, has raised $22 million in Series B funding, bringing its total funding to $50 million.

by
Ben Murray
Updated May 07, 2026

Vori, a San Francisco, CA-based AI grocery startup, has raised $22 million in Series B funding, bringing its total funding to $50 million.  

Investors  

The round was led by Cherryrock Capital, with participation from Greylock Partners and The Factory.  

Cherryrock Capital is a U.S.-based venture capital firm founded in 2023. The firm focuses on Series A and B investments in software and technology startups, particularly supporting founders from historically underfunded backgrounds. Its investment strategy centers on enterprise software, AI, fintech, and healthcare technology companies.  

Vori Use of Funds  

The company will use the funding to expand its AI-powered grocery operating system and modernize outdated grocery store infrastructure, including payments, inventory, ordering, and invoice management.
